university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the underlined letters in FOOD.
university.explanation: FOOD has the long /uː/ sound, the same as in 'hooves'.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the underlined letters in THROAT.
university.explanation: THROAT has the voiceless /θ/ sound, as in 'smith'.

university.subject: English
university.text: _____ is the owner of this pair of scissors?

university.subject: English
university.text: The media house should employ both the novice and the ____ journalists.
university.explanation: 'Veteran' is the opposite of 'novice'.

university.subject: English
university.text: In the sentence 'WHY were you absent yesterday?', the capitalized word is an example of ______

university.subject: English
university.text: The principal sent them home because they did not pay _____ school fees.

university.subject: English
university.text: The business tycoon's daughter was carried _____ by the flattery of her suitor.
university.explanation: 'Carried away' means overwhelmed by emotion.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that best completes the sentence. One of his close friends _____ a soldier.
university.explanation: 'One' is singular, so the verb is 'is'.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to PRAGMATIC.
university.explanation: Pragmatic means practical and realistic; its opposite is 'idealistic'.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the most appropriate interpretation. 'The two young couple took to the altar yesterday' means the two ____
university.explanation: 'Took to the altar' is an idiom for getting married.

university.subject: English
university.text: Usually, Joseph is articulate in his speech, but today he sounds _____
university.explanation: 'Incoherent' is the opposite of 'articulate'.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that best completes the sentence. I found my mother eating _____ the sofa.

university.subject: English
university.text: To _____ an audience is also considered a goal in speech writing.

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. Many go to school _____ bus.
university.explanation: 'By bus' indicates means of transport.

university.subject: English
university.text: In the sentence 'YOU are invited to the party', the capitalized word is an example of ______

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: If the age of a man of 64 years is written as 71 years, calculate the percentage error to 3 significant figures.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: A varies inversely as b. a = 15 when b = 8. Find the value of the constant.
university.explanation: For inverse variation, k = a x b = 15 x 8 = 120.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: The scores of 50 students in a Physics test are: 2 (5 students), 3 (8 students), 4 (12 students), 5 (10 students), 6 (9 students), 7 (6 students). If the pass mark is 5, how many students passed the test?
university.explanation: Scores of 5 and above: 10 + 9 + 6 = 25 students.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: Seven students are to be seated together. Find the number of ways they can be arranged.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: The graph obtained when class frequencies are plotted against class marks is known as a _____
university.explanation: Frequencies against class marks form a frequency polygon, a type of line graph.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: Amphoteric oxides are oxides which _____

options.A: react with water to form oxides
options.B: react with water to form alkali
options.C: show neither acidic nor basic properties

university.explanation: Amphoteric oxides react with both acids and bases.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Basic nuclear radiation was discovered by _____
university.explanation: Henri Becquerel discovered radioactivity in 1896.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: The region of the atmosphere extending from about 75km to about 400km is known as the _____

university.subject: Physics
university.text: The sun is a _____

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: Nitrogen is prepared on a large scale by the _____
university.explanation: Industrial nitrogen is obtained by fractional distillation of liquefied air.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: To reduce heat loss in a transformer:

options.A: the number of turns should be increased
options.B: the soft iron core should be laminated
options.C: the area of the coils should be increased

university.explanation: Laminating the core reduces eddy-current heat losses.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of natural radioactivity?

options.B: it is exhibited only by elements of mass number greater than 206
options.C: the radioactivity of an element is affected by electric and magnetic fields in the surroundings
options.D: radioactive substances emit alpha, beta and gamma radiation

university.explanation: Radioactivity is a nuclear process unaffected by external fields, so that statement is false.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Free electrons in a metallic conductor are best described as electrons that _____

options.A: are tightly bound to the nucleus of each atom
options.B: are detached from their parent atoms and free to move through the conductor
options.C: exist only in a vacuum and cannot move in metals

university.explanation: Free electrons are loosely held and drift to carry current.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: In a series RLC circuit operating at resonance, the resistance is 200 ohms and the peak current is 0.1 A. What is the peak voltage across the resistor?
university.explanation: At resonance the impedance equals R, so V = IR = 0.1 x 200 = 20 V.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: A wire carrying a current tends to move when placed in a magnetic field not parallel to it, in accordance with _____
university.explanation: Fleming's left hand rule gives the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: The main sensory receptors of the body include _____

options.A: tasting, sighting, touching and hearing receptors

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Increase in blood sugar level stimulates the production of _____
university.explanation: Insulin lowers blood glucose levels.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: The topmost layer of the skin that consists of flat dead cells is known as the _____
university.explanation: The cornified layer (stratum corneum) is made of flat dead cells.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Chemicals are stimuli of the nose and the _____
university.explanation: The nose and tongue are chemoreceptors.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Which of the following blood groups is a general donor?
university.explanation: Group O is the universal donor.

university.subject: RSU Rules
university.text: The students' results considered and approved by the Faculty Board of Examiners are presented to the Dean of the Faculty.

university.subject: RSU Rules
university.text: All examination scripts are to be marked and submitted to the Head of Department normally within _____ of a given examination.

university.subject: RSU Rules
university.text: All results approved by the Faculty Board are submitted to the Senate Committee on Assessment and Graduation Requirements within _____ of the last date of the re-examination for approval.

university.subject: RSU Rules
university.text: A student who does not register all his courses within the period stipulated will be made to _____

options.A: forfeit 5% of his total class attendance in all courses
options.B: pay a specific sum for late registration
options.D: suffer any penalty prescribed by the Head of Department

university.subject: RSU Rules
university.text: What is the likely punishment for a student who fails to sit down while invigilators are retrieving scripts at the end of an examination?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: The separation of powers simply means _____

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: A 70-year anniversary is called the _____ anniversary.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What does the acronym ICAO stand for?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who was Nigeria's first indigenous Inspector-General of Police?
university.explanation: Louis Orok Edet was Nigeria's first indigenous IGP, serving from 1964 to 1966.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who is the current Sultan of Sokoto Caliphate?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who is the current Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria?
university.explanation: Olayemi Cardoso became CBN Governor in October 2023.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who is Nigeria's current Minister of Education?
university.explanation: Tunji Alausa was appointed Minister of Education in October 2024.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What does the acronym ECOMOG mean?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Which is the highest mountain in Africa?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: When did Nigeria break diplomatic relations with Israel?
university.explanation: Nigeria broke ties with Israel in 1973 following the Yom Kippur War.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word OPPOSITE IN MEANING to the word in capital letters. The birthday party took place on a DULL day.
university.explanation: "Dull" means lacking brightness or cheer; the opposite is "bright".

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. The student took a correspondence course as a means ___ his knowledge.
university.explanation: The answer key gives "to improve" as the correct completion: "a means to improve his knowledge".

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that is NEAREST IN MEANING to the words written in capital letters. Most of the time, my boss tells COCK-AND-BULL stories.
university.explanation: A "cock-and-bull story" is an absurd, unbelievable, or untrue tale.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that has the SAME STRESS PATTERN as the given word. HONOUR.
university.explanation: HONour is stressed on the first syllable; "human" is also stressed on the first syllable.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose from options A-D the one that contains the sound segment represented by the given phonetic symbol. /u/
university.explanation: According to the answer key, "push" is the option that contains the /u/ sound represented by the symbol.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. EAT
university.explanation: "Eat" and "seat" both contain the long /iː/ vowel sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose from the options the one that has the sound represented by the given phonetic symbol /ou/.
university.explanation: "Rover" contains the /oʊ/ diphthong sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound(s) as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. PROPOSE
university.explanation: The "p" in "propose" is pronounced; "rupture" also contains the /p/ sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that has the same consonant sound as the sound represented by the underlined letter. TEACHER
university.explanation: The answer key indicates option C (deviate) as the correct answer for the consonant sound tested.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letter. STUBBLE
university.explanation: "Stubble" and "tub" both contain the short /ʌ/ vowel sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the correct option. Lack of familiarity with the ___ can prevent a speech presenter from maintaining eye contact with an audience being addressed.
university.explanation: Knowing the speech text well allows the presenter to look at the audience instead of reading notes.

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with correct option. It is most ___ that despite the huge amount spent on health care, most hospitals lack basic medical equipment.
university.explanation: "Astonishing" expresses surprise that so much money is spent yet basic equipment is missing.

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. The woman walked ___ fast that the little girl couldn't catch up with her.
university.explanation: The correct structure is "so ... that" to show result.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that is nearest in meaning to the word written in capital in the sentence below. Over reliance on importation often HAMPERS economic growth.
university.explanation: To hamper means to hinder or restrain progress.

university.subject: English
university.text: From the option lettered A to D, choose the one that has the correct stress pattern. BENEFICIAL
university.explanation: BENEFICIAL is stressed on the third syllable: ben-e-FI-cial.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: The table is a Punnett square for sex determination in man. Which of the following determines a normal male offspring?
university.explanation: A normal human male has the sex chromosomes XY.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Human height is an example of a feature which depends on both ___.
university.explanation: Height is influenced by genes inherited and environmental factors such as nutrition.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Which one is not an example of a cypsela?
university.explanation: Obeche is a hardwood tree, not a member of the Asteraceae family that produces cypsela fruits.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: The gradual, cumulative, adaptive and heritable changes in an organism over a long period of time resulting in emergence of new species is known as ___.
university.explanation: Organic evolution refers to long-term heritable changes leading to new species.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: One of the disadvantages of cross pollination is the ___.

options.A: it leads to less production of seeds
options.B: it produces unhealthy seeds and fruits
options.C: pollen grains are likely to be wasted during the process
options.D: the seeds produced gradually develop into weaker plants

university.explanation: In cross pollination, much pollen may fail to reach a compatible flower and is wasted.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: Factor: x + y - ax - ay.
university.explanation: Group: (x + y) - a(x + y) = (x + y)(1 - a).

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: A pie chart has two equal small sectors and one sector twice each small sector. What is the angle of a small sector?
university.explanation: According to the answer key, the angle of the small sector is 18°.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: Expand [4n - 6m] x [10n + 4m].

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: On a certain project the only grades awarded were 80 and 100. If 10 students completed the project and the average of their grades was 94, how many earned 100?
university.explanation: Let x students score 100. Then 100x + 80(10 - x) = 940, giving 20x = 140, so x = 7.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: A trader buys some eggs at N6 each. She finds that six of them are broken, she sells the rest at N10 each and makes a profit of N160. How many eggs did she buy?
university.explanation: Let x be the number of eggs. Profit = 10(x - 6) - 6x = 4x - 60 = 160, so x = 55.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: The four main sources of raw materials for the plastics and synthetic organic chemical industries are ___, ___, ___ and ___.
university.explanation: Coal, limestone, cellulose and molasses are major raw-material sources for synthetic organic industries.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: A positive brown ring test indicates the presence of ___.
university.explanation: The brown ring test is used to detect nitrate ions (NO₃⁻).

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: Which of the following oxides of nitrogen is unstable in air?
university.explanation: The answer key gives dinitrogen pentoxide (N₂O₅) as the oxide of nitrogen that is unstable in air.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: The production of plastics involves these conditions with the exception of ___.
university.explanation: Plastic production generally uses high temperature, high pressure and setting/catalysts, not low temperature.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: If sulphur is dissolved in carbon (IV) sulphide and the solution allowed to evaporate, the allotrope of sulphur formed is ___.
university.explanation: Slow evaporation of a sulphur solution in CS₂ yields rhombic sulphur crystals.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: The following is a method of producing spectra of hot substances by ___.

options.B: direct application of a Bunsen flame
options.C: use of two metal electrodes connected to the secondary of an induction coil
options.D: use of two non-metal rods connected to a dc voltage

university.explanation: According to the answer key, option D is the correct method described.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: X-rays can be used in the study of crystal structure because they ___.
university.explanation: X-rays have very short wavelengths comparable to atomic spacing in crystals, allowing diffraction studies.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Which of the following is an advantage of the capacitor in an induction coil?

options.A: the presence of the capacitor is to slow down the fall of current in the primary coil at the instant of break
options.B: to produce low voltage from a high voltage for use
options.C: to provide sparks required to ignite the petrol air mixture in car cylinder
options.D: this is to prevent the induced emf in the primary coil from rising as to cause unwanted spark

university.explanation: The answer key gives option B as the advantage of the capacitor in an induction coil.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Lines of force (i) are continuous in any region with charges (ii) cross each other (iii) indicate the direction of the field (iv) start and end on negative charge only. Which of the statements above are NOT correct?
university.explanation: Lines of force never cross, and they start on positive and end on negative charges.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: In an alternating circuit theory, the units of impedance, r.m.s voltage and resonance frequency are respectively equal to ___.
university.explanation: Impedance is measured in ohms, voltage in volts, and frequency in hertz.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: The head of Department, Dean of Faculty, Faculty Examination Officer, and Secretary to the Faculty Board retain copies of the results of all students to avoid unofficial releases of results.
university.explanation: According to the answer key, this statement is true.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: The departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the ___.
university.explanation: The answer key indicates that the departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the faculty examination officer.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: A student in a four-year degree programme in the Rivers State University who is unable to complete his programme in 12 semesters may be allowed an additional ___ semesters, but shall be eligible to a ___ degree.
university.explanation: The answer key gives 2 additional semesters and a Pass degree.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: The Rivers State University is the only university in Nigeria that offers first degree programmes in ___ and ___.

options.C: land surveying and aeronautical telecommunication engineering

university.explanation: According to the answer key, RSU uniquely offers first degree programmes in marine engineering and secretarial administration.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: What is the likely punishment meted on a student who destroys possible evidence for an examination malpractice?
university.explanation: Destroying evidence of examination malpractice is a serious offence that typically leads to dismissal.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Which country was the last to win independence from colonial rule?
university.explanation: According to the answer key, Angola is the correct choice among the options given.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: A country made up of semi-autonomous units is called ___.
university.explanation: A confederation is a union of semi-autonomous states or units.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What is the ultimate aim of political parties?

options.B: to be opposition to the government in power
options.C: to train citizens on political participation

university.explanation: Political parties are primarily formed to acquire and exercise political power.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What system of Government is political power inherited?
university.explanation: In a monarchy, political power is usually inherited.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: “But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of ___”
university.explanation: Jesus sent his disciples to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: The following are qualities of a good citizen except a good citizen should ___.

options.A: pay taxes, rates, levies and electricity bills
options.B: question all orders constituted by the authorities before carrying them out
options.C: go out to cast his vote at elections
options.D: learn to express his grievances through dialogue and not through violence

university.explanation: A good citizen obeys lawful authority; questioning every order before obeying is the exception.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: How did God convince Jeremiah of his divine call?

options.B: He spoke to him directly from heaven
options.C: He sent a prophet to inform him
options.E: He gave him a vision of a burning bush

university.explanation: God touched Jeremiah’s mouth and put his words into it.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: Paul considered himself unfit to be called an apostle because he ___.

options.B: was the least among the Jewish race
options.E: was not one of the twelve disciples

university.explanation: Paul persecuted the church before his conversion, so he felt unworthy of the title apostle.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: “I will; be clean”, Jesus made this statement at the healing of ___.

options.A: the woman with the issue of blood

university.explanation: Jesus healed a leper and said, “I will; be clean.”

university.subject: CRS
university.text: The close of the Old Testament and the beginning of the New Testament is called the ___ period.
university.explanation: According to the answer key, this period is called the eschatological period.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the underlined letters in FOOD.
university.explanation: FOOD has the long /uː/ sound, the same as in 'hooves'.

university.text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the underlined letters in THROAT.
university.explanation: THROAT has the voiceless /θ/ sound, as in 'smith'.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who was Nigeria's first indigenous Inspector-General of Police?
university.explanation: Louis Orok Edet was Nigeria's first indigenous IGP, serving from 1964 to 1966.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who is the current Sultan of Sokoto Caliphate?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who is the current Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria?
university.explanation: Olayemi Cardoso became CBN Governor in October 2023.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Who is Nigeria's current Minister of Education?
university.explanation: Tunji Alausa was appointed Minister of Education in October 2024.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What does the acronym ECOMOG mean?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Which is the highest mountain in Africa?

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: When did Nigeria break diplomatic relations with Israel?
university.explanation: Nigeria broke ties with Israel in 1973 following the Yom Kippur War.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word OPPOSITE IN MEANING to the word in capital letters. The birthday party took place on a DULL day.
university.explanation: "Dull" means lacking brightness or cheer; the opposite is "bright".

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. The student took a correspondence course as a means ___ his knowledge.
university.explanation: The answer key gives "to improve" as the correct completion: "a means to improve his knowledge".

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that is NEAREST IN MEANING to the words written in capital letters. Most of the time, my boss tells COCK-AND-BULL stories.
university.explanation: A "cock-and-bull story" is an absurd, unbelievable, or untrue tale.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that has the SAME STRESS PATTERN as the given word. HONOUR.
university.explanation: HONour is stressed on the first syllable; "human" is also stressed on the first syllable.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose from options A-D the one that contains the sound segment represented by the given phonetic symbol. /u/
university.explanation: According to the answer key, "push" is the option that contains the /u/ sound represented by the symbol.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. EAT
university.explanation: "Eat" and "seat" both contain the long /iː/ vowel sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose from the options the one that has the sound represented by the given phonetic symbol /ou/.
university.explanation: "Rover" contains the /oʊ/ diphthong sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound(s) as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. PROPOSE
university.explanation: The "p" in "propose" is pronounced; "rupture" also contains the /p/ sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that has the same consonant sound as the sound represented by the underlined letter. TEACHER
university.explanation: The answer key indicates option C (deviate) as the correct answer for the consonant sound tested.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letter. STUBBLE
university.explanation: "Stubble" and "tub" both contain the short /ʌ/ vowel sound.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the correct option. Lack of familiarity with the ___ can prevent a speech presenter from maintaining eye contact with an audience being addressed.
university.explanation: Knowing the speech text well allows the presenter to look at the audience instead of reading notes.

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with correct option. It is most ___ that despite the huge amount spent on health care, most hospitals lack basic medical equipment.
university.explanation: "Astonishing" expresses surprise that so much money is spent yet basic equipment is missing.

university.subject: English
university.text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. The woman walked ___ fast that the little girl couldn't catch up with her.
university.explanation: The correct structure is "so ... that" to show result.

university.subject: English
university.text: Choose the one that is nearest in meaning to the word written in capital in the sentence below. Over reliance on importation often HAMPERS economic growth.
university.explanation: To hamper means to hinder or restrain progress.

university.subject: English
university.text: From the option lettered A to D, choose the one that has the correct stress pattern. BENEFICIAL
university.explanation: BENEFICIAL is stressed on the third syllable: ben-e-FI-cial.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: The table is a Punnett square for sex determination in man. Which of the following determines a normal male offspring?
university.explanation: A normal human male has the sex chromosomes XY.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Human height is an example of a feature which depends on both ___.
university.explanation: Height is influenced by genes inherited and environmental factors such as nutrition.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: Which one is not an example of a cypsela?
university.explanation: Obeche is a hardwood tree, not a member of the Asteraceae family that produces cypsela fruits.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: The gradual, cumulative, adaptive and heritable changes in an organism over a long period of time resulting in emergence of new species is known as ___.
university.explanation: Organic evolution refers to long-term heritable changes leading to new species.

university.subject: Biology
university.text: One of the disadvantages of cross pollination is the ___.

options.A: it leads to less production of seeds
options.B: it produces unhealthy seeds and fruits
options.C: pollen grains are likely to be wasted during the process
options.D: the seeds produced gradually develop into weaker plants

university.explanation: In cross pollination, much pollen may fail to reach a compatible flower and is wasted.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: Factor: x + y - ax - ay.
university.explanation: Group: (x + y) - a(x + y) = (x + y)(1 - a).

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: A pie chart has two equal small sectors and one sector twice each small sector. What is the angle of a small sector?
university.explanation: According to the answer key, the angle of the small sector is 18°.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: Expand [4n - 6m] x [10n + 4m].

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: On a certain project the only grades awarded were 80 and 100. If 10 students completed the project and the average of their grades was 94, how many earned 100?
university.explanation: Let x students score 100. Then 100x + 80(10 - x) = 940, giving 20x = 140, so x = 7.

university.subject: Mathematics
university.text: A trader buys some eggs at N6 each. She finds that six of them are broken, she sells the rest at N10 each and makes a profit of N160. How many eggs did she buy?
university.explanation: Let x be the number of eggs. Profit = 10(x - 6) - 6x = 4x - 60 = 160, so x = 55.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: The four main sources of raw materials for the plastics and synthetic organic chemical industries are ___, ___, ___ and ___.
university.explanation: Coal, limestone, cellulose and molasses are major raw-material sources for synthetic organic industries.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: A positive brown ring test indicates the presence of ___.
university.explanation: The brown ring test is used to detect nitrate ions (NO₃⁻).

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: Which of the following oxides of nitrogen is unstable in air?
university.explanation: The answer key gives dinitrogen pentoxide (N₂O₅) as the oxide of nitrogen that is unstable in air.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: The production of plastics involves these conditions with the exception of ___.
university.explanation: Plastic production generally uses high temperature, high pressure and setting/catalysts, not low temperature.

university.subject: Chemistry
university.text: If sulphur is dissolved in carbon (IV) sulphide and the solution allowed to evaporate, the allotrope of sulphur formed is ___.
university.explanation: Slow evaporation of a sulphur solution in CS₂ yields rhombic sulphur crystals.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: The following is a method of producing spectra of hot substances by ___.

options.B: direct application of a Bunsen flame
options.C: use of two metal electrodes connected to the secondary of an induction coil
options.D: use of two non-metal rods connected to a dc voltage

university.explanation: According to the answer key, option D is the correct method described.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: X-rays can be used in the study of crystal structure because they ___.
university.explanation: X-rays have very short wavelengths comparable to atomic spacing in crystals, allowing diffraction studies.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Which of the following is an advantage of the capacitor in an induction coil?

options.A: the presence of the capacitor is to slow down the fall of current in the primary coil at the instant of break
options.B: to produce low voltage from a high voltage for use
options.C: to provide sparks required to ignite the petrol air mixture in car cylinder
options.D: this is to prevent the induced emf in the primary coil from rising as to cause unwanted spark

university.explanation: The answer key gives option B as the advantage of the capacitor in an induction coil.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: Lines of force (i) are continuous in any region with charges (ii) cross each other (iii) indicate the direction of the field (iv) start and end on negative charge only. Which of the statements above are NOT correct?
university.explanation: Lines of force never cross, and they start on positive and end on negative charges.

university.subject: Physics
university.text: In an alternating circuit theory, the units of impedance, r.m.s voltage and resonance frequency are respectively equal to ___.
university.explanation: Impedance is measured in ohms, voltage in volts, and frequency in hertz.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: The head of Department, Dean of Faculty, Faculty Examination Officer, and Secretary to the Faculty Board retain copies of the results of all students to avoid unofficial releases of results.
university.explanation: According to the answer key, this statement is true.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: The departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the ___.
university.explanation: The answer key indicates that the departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the faculty examination officer.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: A student in a four-year degree programme in the Rivers State University who is unable to complete his programme in 12 semesters may be allowed an additional ___ semesters, but shall be eligible to a ___ degree.
university.explanation: The answer key gives 2 additional semesters and a Pass degree.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: The Rivers State University is the only university in Nigeria that offers first degree programmes in ___ and ___.

options.C: land surveying and aeronautical telecommunication engineering

university.explanation: According to the answer key, RSU uniquely offers first degree programmes in marine engineering and secretarial administration.

university.subject: About RSU
university.text: What is the likely punishment meted on a student who destroys possible evidence for an examination malpractice?
university.explanation: Destroying evidence of examination malpractice is a serious offence that typically leads to dismissal.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: Which country was the last to win independence from colonial rule?
university.explanation: According to the answer key, Angola is the correct choice among the options given.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: A country made up of semi-autonomous units is called ___.
university.explanation: A confederation is a union of semi-autonomous states or units.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What is the ultimate aim of political parties?

options.B: to be opposition to the government in power
options.C: to train citizens on political participation

university.explanation: Political parties are primarily formed to acquire and exercise political power.

university.subject: Current Affairs
university.text: What system of Government is political power inherited?
university.explanation: In a monarchy, political power is usually inherited.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: “But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of ___”
university.explanation: Jesus sent his disciples to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: The following are qualities of a good citizen except a good citizen should ___.

options.A: pay taxes, rates, levies and electricity bills
options.B: question all orders constituted by the authorities before carrying them out
options.C: go out to cast his vote at elections
options.D: learn to express his grievances through dialogue and not through violence

university.explanation: A good citizen obeys lawful authority; questioning every order before obeying is the exception.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: How did God convince Jeremiah of his divine call?

options.B: He spoke to him directly from heaven
options.C: He sent a prophet to inform him
options.E: He gave him a vision of a burning bush

university.explanation: God touched Jeremiah’s mouth and put his words into it.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: Paul considered himself unfit to be called an apostle because he ___.

options.B: was the least among the Jewish race
options.E: was not one of the twelve disciples

university.explanation: Paul persecuted the church before his conversion, so he felt unworthy of the title apostle.

university.subject: CRS
university.text: “I will; be clean”, Jesus made this statement at the healing of ___.

options.A: the woman with the issue of blood

university.explanation: Jesus healed a leper and said, “I will; be clean.”

university.subject: CRS
university.text: The close of the Old Testament and the beginning of the New Testament is called the ___ period.
university.explanation: According to the answer key, this period is called the eschatological period.

university: Sad is the opposite of happy.
Format these into the import CSV, year = 20XX.
Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the underlined letters in FOOD.
FOOD has the long /uː/ sound, the same as in 'hooves'.
Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the underlined letters in THROAT.
THROAT has the voiceless /θ/ sound, as in 'smith'.
_____ is the owner of this pair of scissors?
The media house should employ both the novice and the ____ journalists.
In the sentence 'WHY were you absent yesterday?', the capitalized word is an example of ______
The principal sent them home because they did not pay _____ school fees.
The business tycoon's daughter was carried _____ by the flattery of her suitor.
Choose the word that best completes the sentence. One of his close friends _____ a soldier.
'One' is singular, so the verb is 'is'.
Choose the word most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to PRAGMATIC.
Pragmatic means practical and realistic; its opposite is 'idealistic'.
Choose the most appropriate interpretation. 'The two young couple took to the altar yesterday' means the two ____
'Took to the altar' is an idiom for getting married.
Usually, Joseph is articulate in his speech, but today he sounds _____
Choose the word that best completes the sentence. I found my mother eating _____ the sofa.
To _____ an audience is also considered a goal in speech writing.
Fill in the blank space with the correct option. Many go to school _____ bus.
In the sentence 'YOU are invited to the party', the capitalized word is an example of ______
If the age of a man of 64 years is written as 71 years, calculate the percentage error to 3 significant figures.
A varies inversely as b. a = 15 when b = 8. Find the value of the constant.
For inverse variation, k = a x b = 15 x 8 = 120.

The scores of 50 students in a Physics test are: 2 (5 students), 3 (8 students), 4 (12 students), 5 (10 students), 6 (9 students), 7 (6 students). If the pass mark is 5, how many students passed the test?
Seven students are to be seated together. Find the number of ways they can be arranged.
The graph obtained when class frequencies are plotted against class marks is known as a _____
Frequencies against class marks form a frequency polygon, a type of line graph.
react with water to form oxides
react with water to form alkali
show neither acidic nor basic properties
Amphoteric oxides react with both acids and bases.
Basic nuclear radiation was discovered by _____
The region of the atmosphere extending from about 75km to about 400km is known as the _____
Nitrogen is prepared on a large scale by the _____
Industrial nitrogen is obtained by fractional distillation of liquefied air.
To reduce heat loss in a transformer:
the number of turns should be increased
the soft iron core should be laminated
the area of the coils should be increased
Laminating the core reduces eddy-current heat losses.
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of natural radioactivity?
it is exhibited only by elements of mass number greater than 206
the radioactivity of an element is affected by electric and magnetic fields in the surroundings
radioactive substances emit alpha, beta and gamma radiation
Radioactivity is a nuclear process unaffected by external fields, so that statement is false.
Free electrons in a metallic conductor are best described as electrons that _____
are tightly bound to the nucleus of each atom
are detached from their parent atoms and free to move through the conductor
exist only in a vacuum and cannot move in metals
Free electrons are loosely held and drift to carry current.
In a series RLC circuit operating at resonance, the resistance is 200 ohms and the peak current is 0.1 A. What is the peak voltage across the resistor?
At resonance the impedance equals R, so V = IR = 0.1 x 200 = 20 V.
A wire carrying a current tends to move when placed in a magnetic field not parallel to it, in accordance with _____
Fleming's left hand rule gives the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor.
The main sensory receptors of the body include _____
tasting, sighting, touching and hearing receptors
Increase in blood sugar level stimulates the production of _____
The topmost layer of the skin that consists of flat dead cells is known as the _____
The cornified layer (stratum corneum) is made of flat dead cells.
Chemicals are stimuli of the nose and the _____
The nose and tongue are chemoreceptors.
Which of the following blood groups is a general donor?
Group O is the universal donor.
The students' results considered and approved by the Faculty Board of Examiners are presented to the Dean of the Faculty.
All examination scripts are to be marked and submitted to the Head of Department normally within _____ of a given examination.
All results approved by the Faculty Board are submitted to the Senate Committee on Assessment and Graduation Requirements within _____ of the last date of the re-examination for approval.
A student who does not register all his courses within the period stipulated will be made to _____
forfeit 5% of his total class attendance in all courses
pay a specific sum for late registration
suffer any penalty prescribed by the Head of Department
What is the likely punishment for a student who fails to sit down while invigilators are retrieving scripts at the end of an examination?
The separation of powers simply means _____
A 70-year anniversary is called the _____ anniversary.
What does the acronym ICAO stand for?
Who was Nigeria's first indigenous Inspector-General of Police?
Louis Orok Edet was Nigeria's first indigenous IGP, serving from 1964 to 1966.
Who is the current Sultan of Sokoto Caliphate?
Who is the current Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria?
Olayemi Cardoso became CBN Governor in October 2023.
Who is Nigeria's current Minister of Education?
Tunji Alausa was appointed Minister of Education in October 2024.
What does the acronym ECOMOG mean?
Which is the highest mountain in Africa?
When did Nigeria break diplomatic relations with Israel?
Nigeria broke ties with Israel in 1973 following the Yom Kippur War.

university.question: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the underlined letters in FOOD.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: FOOD has the long /uː/ sound, the same as in 'hooves'.

university.question: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the underlined letters in THROAT.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: THROAT has the voiceless /θ/ sound, as in 'smith'.

university.question: _____ is the owner of this pair of scissors?
university.subject: English

university.question: The media house should employ both the novice and the ____ journalists.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: 'Veteran' is the opposite of 'novice'.

university.question: In the sentence 'WHY were you absent yesterday?', the capitalized word is an example of ______
university.subject: English

university.question: The principal sent them home because they did not pay _____ school fees.
university.subject: English

university.question: The business tycoon's daughter was carried _____ by the flattery of her suitor.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: 'Carried away' means overwhelmed by emotion.

university.question: Choose the word that best completes the sentence. One of his close friends _____ a soldier.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: 'One' is singular, so the verb is 'is'.

university.question: Choose the word most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to PRAGMATIC.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: Pragmatic means practical and realistic; its opposite is 'idealistic'.

university.question: Choose the most appropriate interpretation. 'The two young couple took to the altar yesterday' means the two ____
university.subject: English
university.explanation: 'Took to the altar' is an idiom for getting married.

university.question: Usually, Joseph is articulate in his speech, but today he sounds _____
university.subject: English
university.explanation: 'Incoherent' is the opposite of 'articulate'.

university.question: Choose the word that best completes the sentence. I found my mother eating _____ the sofa.
university.subject: English

university.question: To _____ an audience is also considered a goal in speech writing.
university.subject: English

university.question: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. Many go to school _____ bus.
university.subject: English
university.explanation: 'By bus' indicates means of transport.

university.question: In the sentence 'YOU are invited to the party', the capitalized word is an example of ______
university.subject: English

university.question: If the age of a man of 64 years is written as 71 years, calculate the percentage error to 3 significant figures.
university.subject: Mathematics

university.question: A varies inversely as b. a = 15 when b = 8. Find the value of the constant.
university.subject: Mathematics
university.explanation: For inverse variation, k = a x b = 15 x 8 = 120.

university.question: The scores of 50 students in a Physics test are: 2 (5 students), 3 (8 students), 4 (12 students), 5 (10 students), 6 (9 students), 7 (6 students). If the pass mark is 5, how many students passed the test?
university.subject: Mathematics
university.explanation: Scores of 5 and above: 10 + 9 + 6 = 25 students.

university.question: Seven students are to be seated together. Find the number of ways they can be arranged.
university.subject: Mathematics

university.question: The graph obtained when class frequencies are plotted against class marks is known as a _____
university.subject: Mathematics
university.explanation: Frequencies against class marks form a frequency polygon, a type of line graph.

university.question: Amphoteric oxides are oxides which _____
options: react with water to form oxides
react with water to form alkali
show neither acidic nor basic properties
university.subject: Chemistry
university.explanation: Amphoteric oxides react with both acids and bases.

university.question: Basic nuclear radiation was discovered by _____
university.subject: Physics
university.explanation: Henri Becquerel discovered radioactivity in 1896.

university.question: The region of the atmosphere extending from about 75km to about 400km is known as the _____
university.subject: Physics

university.question: The sun is a _____
university.subject: Physics

university.question: Nitrogen is prepared on a large scale by the _____
university.subject: Chemistry
university.explanation: Industrial nitrogen is obtained by fractional distillation of liquefied air.

university.question: To reduce heat loss in a transformer:
options: the number of turns should be increased
the soft iron core should be laminated
the area of the coils should be increased
university.subject: Physics
university.explanation: Laminating the core reduces eddy-current heat losses.

university.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of natural radioactivity?
options: it is exhibited only by elements of mass number greater than 206
the radioactivity of an element is affected by electric and magnetic fields in the surroundings
radioactive substances emit alpha, beta and gamma radiation
university.subject: Physics
university.explanation: Radioactivity is a nuclear process unaffected by external fields, so that statement is false.

university.question: Free electrons in a metallic conductor are best described as electrons that _____
options: are tightly bound to the nucleus of each atom
are detached from their parent atoms and free to move through the conductor
exist only in a vacuum and cannot move in metals
university.subject: Physics
university.explanation: Free electrons are loosely held and drift to carry current.

university.question: In a series RLC circuit operating at resonance, the resistance is 200 ohms and the peak current is 0.1 A. What is the peak voltage across the resistor?
university.subject: Physics
university.explanation: At resonance the impedance equals R, so V = IR = 0.1 x 200 = 20 V.

university.question: A wire carrying a current tends to move when placed in a magnetic field not parallel to it, in accordance with _____
university.subject: Physics
university.explanation: Fleming's left hand rule gives the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor.

university.question: The main sensory receptors of the body include _____
options: tasting, sighting, touching and hearing receptors
university.subject: Biology

university.question: Increase in blood sugar level stimulates the production of _____
university.subject: Biology
university.explanation: Insulin lowers blood glucose levels.

university.question: The topmost layer of the skin that consists of flat dead cells is known as the _____
university.subject: Biology
university.explanation: The cornified layer (stratum corneum) is made of flat dead cells.

university.question: Chemicals are stimuli of the nose and the _____
university.subject: Biology
university.explanation: The nose and tongue are chemoreceptors.

university.question: Which of the following blood groups is a general donor?
university.subject: Biology
university.explanation: Group O is the universal donor.

university.question: The students' results considered and approved by the Faculty Board of Examiners are presented to the Dean of the Faculty.
university.subject: RSU Rules

university.question: All examination scripts are to be marked and submitted to the Head of Department normally within _____ of a given examination.
university.subject: RSU Rules

university.question: All results approved by the Faculty Board are submitted to the Senate Committee on Assessment and Graduation Requirements within _____ of the last date of the re-examination for approval.
university.subject: RSU Rules

university.question: A student who does not register all his courses within the period stipulated will be made to _____
options: forfeit 5% of his total class attendance in all courses
pay a specific sum for late registration
suffer any penalty prescribed by the Head of Department
university.subject: RSU Rules

university.question: What is the likely punishment for a student who fails to sit down while invigilators are retrieving scripts at the end of an examination?
university.subject: RSU Rules

university.question: The separation of powers simply means _____
university.subject: Current Affairs

university.question: A 70-year anniversary is called the _____ anniversary.
university.subject: Current Affairs

university.subject: Current Affairs

university.question: Who was Nigeria's first indigenous Inspector-General of Police?
university.subject: Current Affairs
university.explanation: Louis Orok Edet was Nigeria's first indigenous IGP, serving from 1964 to 1966.

university.subject: Current Affairs

university.question: Who is the current Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria?
university.subject: Current Affairs
university.explanation: Olayemi Cardoso became CBN Governor in October 2023.

university.question: Who is Nigeria's current Minister of Education?
university.subject: Current Affairs
university.explanation: Tunji Alausa was appointed Minister of Education in October 2024.

university.question: What does the acronym ECOMOG mean?
university.subject: Current Affairs

university.question: Which is the highest mountain in Africa?
university.subject: Current Affairs

university.question: When did Nigeria break diplomatic relations with Israel?
university.subject: Current Affairs
university.explanation: Nigeria broke ties with Israel in 1973 following the Yom Kippur War.

subject: English
text: To _____ an audience is also considered a goal in speech writing.

subject: English
text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. Many go to school _____ bus.
explanation: 'By bus' indicates means of transport.

subject: English
text: In the sentence 'YOU are invited to the party', the capitalized word is an example of ______

subject: Mathematics
text: If the age of a man of 64 years is written as 71 years, calculate the percentage error to 3 significant figures.

subject: Mathematics
text: A varies inversely as b. a = 15 when b = 8. Find the value of the constant.
explanation: For inverse variation, k = a x b = 15 x 8 = 120.

subject: Mathematics
text: The scores of 50 students in a Physics test are: 2 (5 students), 3 (8 students), 4 (12 students), 5 (10 students), 6 (9 students), 7 (6 students). If the pass mark is 5, how many students passed the test?
explanation: Scores of 5 and above: 10 + 9 + 6 = 25 students.

subject: Mathematics
text: Seven students are to be seated together. Find the number of ways they can be arranged.

subject: Mathematics
text: The graph obtained when class frequencies are plotted against class marks is known as a _____
explanation: Frequencies against class marks form a frequency polygon, a type of line graph.

subject: Chemistry
text: Amphoteric oxides are oxides which _____

options.A: react with water to form oxides
options.B: react with water to form alkali
options.C: show neither acidic nor basic properties

explanation: Amphoteric oxides react with both acids and bases.

subject: Physics
text: Basic nuclear radiation was discovered by _____
explanation: Henri Becquerel discovered radioactivity in 1896.

subject: Physics
text: The region of the atmosphere extending from about 75km to about 400km is known as the _____

subject: Physics
text: The sun is a _____

subject: Chemistry
text: Nitrogen is prepared on a large scale by the _____
explanation: Industrial nitrogen is obtained by fractional distillation of liquefied air.

subject: Physics
text: To reduce heat loss in a transformer:

options.A: the number of turns should be increased
options.B: the soft iron core should be laminated
options.C: the area of the coils should be increased

explanation: Laminating the core reduces eddy-current heat losses.

subject: Physics
text: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of natural radioactivity?

options.B: it is exhibited only by elements of mass number greater than 206
options.C: the radioactivity of an element is affected by electric and magnetic fields in the surroundings
options.D: radioactive substances emit alpha, beta and gamma radiation

explanation: Radioactivity is a nuclear process unaffected by external fields, so that statement is false.

subject: Physics
text: Free electrons in a metallic conductor are best described as electrons that _____

options.A: are tightly bound to the nucleus of each atom
options.B: are detached from their parent atoms and free to move through the conductor
options.C: exist only in a vacuum and cannot move in metals

explanation: Free electrons are loosely held and drift to carry current.

subject: Physics
text: In a series RLC circuit operating at resonance, the resistance is 200 ohms and the peak current is 0.1 A. What is the peak voltage across the resistor?
explanation: At resonance the impedance equals R, so V = IR = 0.1 x 200 = 20 V.

subject: Physics
text: A wire carrying a current tends to move when placed in a magnetic field not parallel to it, in accordance with _____
explanation: Fleming's left hand rule gives the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor.

subject: Biology
text: The main sensory receptors of the body include _____

options.A: tasting, sighting, touching and hearing receptors

subject: Biology
text: Increase in blood sugar level stimulates the production of _____
explanation: Insulin lowers blood glucose levels.

subject: Biology
text: The topmost layer of the skin that consists of flat dead cells is known as the _____
explanation: The cornified layer (stratum corneum) is made of flat dead cells.

subject: Biology
text: Chemicals are stimuli of the nose and the _____
explanation: The nose and tongue are chemoreceptors.

subject: Biology
text: Which of the following blood groups is a general donor?
explanation: Group O is the universal donor.

subject: RSU Rules
text: The students' results considered and approved by the Faculty Board of Examiners are presented to the Dean of the Faculty.

subject: RSU Rules
text: All examination scripts are to be marked and submitted to the Head of Department normally within _____ of a given examination.

subject: RSU Rules
text: All results approved by the Faculty Board are submitted to the Senate Committee on Assessment and Graduation Requirements within _____ of the last date of the re-examination for approval.

subject: RSU Rules
text: A student who does not register all his courses within the period stipulated will be made to _____

options.A: forfeit 5% of his total class attendance in all courses
options.B: pay a specific sum for late registration
options.D: suffer any penalty prescribed by the Head of Department

subject: RSU Rules
text: What is the likely punishment for a student who fails to sit down while invigilators are retrieving scripts at the end of an examination?

subject: Current Affairs
text: The separation of powers simply means _____

subject: Current Affairs
text: A 70-year anniversary is called the _____ anniversary.

subject: Current Affairs
text: What does the acronym ICAO stand for?

subject: Current Affairs
text: Who was Nigeria's first indigenous Inspector-General of Police?
explanation: Louis Orok Edet was Nigeria's first indigenous IGP, serving from 1964 to 1966.

subject: Current Affairs
text: Who is the current Sultan of Sokoto Caliphate?

subject: Current Affairs
text: Who is the current Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria?
explanation: Olayemi Cardoso became CBN Governor in October 2023.

subject: Current Affairs
text: Who is Nigeria's current Minister of Education?
explanation: Tunji Alausa was appointed Minister of Education in October 2024.

subject: Current Affairs
text: What does the acronym ECOMOG mean?

subject: Current Affairs
text: Which is the highest mountain in Africa?

subject: Current Affairs
text: When did Nigeria break diplomatic relations with Israel?
explanation: Nigeria broke ties with Israel in 1973 following the Yom Kippur War.

subject: English
text: Choose the word OPPOSITE IN MEANING to the word in capital letters. The birthday party took place on a DULL day.
explanation: "Dull" means lacking brightness or cheer; the opposite is "bright".

subject: English
text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. The student took a correspondence course as a means ___ his knowledge.
explanation: The answer key gives "to improve" as the correct completion: "a means to improve his knowledge".

subject: English
text: Choose the one that is NEAREST IN MEANING to the words written in capital letters. Most of the time, my boss tells COCK-AND-BULL stories.
explanation: A "cock-and-bull story" is an absurd, unbelievable, or untrue tale.

subject: English
text: Choose the one that has the SAME STRESS PATTERN as the given word. HONOUR.
explanation: HONour is stressed on the first syllable; "human" is also stressed on the first syllable.

subject: English
text: Choose from options A-D the one that contains the sound segment represented by the given phonetic symbol. /u/
explanation: According to the answer key, "push" is the option that contains the /u/ sound represented by the symbol.

subject: English
text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. EAT
explanation: "Eat" and "seat" both contain the long /iː/ vowel sound.

subject: English
text: Choose from the options the one that has the sound represented by the given phonetic symbol /ou/.
explanation: "Rover" contains the /oʊ/ diphthong sound.

subject: English
text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound(s) as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. PROPOSE
explanation: The "p" in "propose" is pronounced; "rupture" also contains the /p/ sound.

subject: English
text: Choose the one that has the same consonant sound as the sound represented by the underlined letter. TEACHER
explanation: The answer key indicates option C (deviate) as the correct answer for the consonant sound tested.

subject: English
text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letter. STUBBLE
explanation: "Stubble" and "tub" both contain the short /ʌ/ vowel sound.

subject: English
text: Choose the correct option. Lack of familiarity with the ___ can prevent a speech presenter from maintaining eye contact with an audience being addressed.
explanation: Knowing the speech text well allows the presenter to look at the audience instead of reading notes.

subject: English
text: Fill in the blank space with correct option. It is most ___ that despite the huge amount spent on health care, most hospitals lack basic medical equipment.
explanation: "Astonishing" expresses surprise that so much money is spent yet basic equipment is missing.

subject: English
text: Fill in the blank space with the correct option. The woman walked ___ fast that the little girl couldn't catch up with her.
explanation: The correct structure is "so ... that" to show result.

subject: English
text: Choose the one that is nearest in meaning to the word written in capital in the sentence below. Over reliance on importation often HAMPERS economic growth.
explanation: To hamper means to hinder or restrain progress.

subject: English
text: From the option lettered A to D, choose the one that has the correct stress pattern. BENEFICIAL
explanation: BENEFICIAL is stressed on the third syllable: ben-e-FI-cial.

subject: Biology
text: The table is a Punnett square for sex determination in man. Which of the following determines a normal male offspring?
explanation: A normal human male has the sex chromosomes XY.

subject: Biology
text: Human height is an example of a feature which depends on both ___.
explanation: Height is influenced by genes inherited and environmental factors such as nutrition.

subject: Biology
text: Which one is not an example of a cypsela?
explanation: Obeche is a hardwood tree, not a member of the Asteraceae family that produces cypsela fruits.

subject: Biology
text: The gradual, cumulative, adaptive and heritable changes in an organism over a long period of time resulting in emergence of new species is known as ___.
explanation: Organic evolution refers to long-term heritable changes leading to new species.

subject: Biology
text: One of the disadvantages of cross pollination is the ___.

options.A: it leads to less production of seeds
options.B: it produces unhealthy seeds and fruits
options.C: pollen grains are likely to be wasted during the process
options.D: the seeds produced gradually develop into weaker plants

explanation: In cross pollination, much pollen may fail to reach a compatible flower and is wasted.

subject: Mathematics
text: Factor: x + y - ax - ay.
explanation: Group: (x + y) - a(x + y) = (x + y)(1 - a).

subject: Mathematics
text: A pie chart has two equal small sectors and one sector twice each small sector. What is the angle of a small sector?
explanation: According to the answer key, the angle of the small sector is 18°.

subject: Mathematics
text: Expand [4n - 6m] x [10n + 4m].

subject: Mathematics
text: On a certain project the only grades awarded were 80 and 100. If 10 students completed the project and the average of their grades was 94, how many earned 100?
explanation: Let x students score 100. Then 100x + 80(10 - x) = 940, giving 20x = 140, so x = 7.

subject: Mathematics
text: A trader buys some eggs at N6 each. She finds that six of them are broken, she sells the rest at N10 each and makes a profit of N160. How many eggs did she buy?
explanation: Let x be the number of eggs. Profit = 10(x - 6) - 6x = 4x - 60 = 160, so x = 55.

subject: Chemistry
text: The four main sources of raw materials for the plastics and synthetic organic chemical industries are ___, ___, ___ and ___.
explanation: Coal, limestone, cellulose and molasses are major raw-material sources for synthetic organic industries.

subject: Chemistry
text: A positive brown ring test indicates the presence of ___.
explanation: The brown ring test is used to detect nitrate ions (NO₃⁻).

subject: Chemistry
text: Which of the following oxides of nitrogen is unstable in air?
explanation: The answer key gives dinitrogen pentoxide (N₂O₅) as the oxide of nitrogen that is unstable in air.

subject: Chemistry
text: The production of plastics involves these conditions with the exception of ___.
explanation: Plastic production generally uses high temperature, high pressure and setting/catalysts, not low temperature.

subject: Chemistry
text: If sulphur is dissolved in carbon (IV) sulphide and the solution allowed to evaporate, the allotrope of sulphur formed is ___.
explanation: Slow evaporation of a sulphur solution in CS₂ yields rhombic sulphur crystals.

subject: Physics
text: The following is a method of producing spectra of hot substances by ___.

options.B: direct application of a Bunsen flame
options.C: use of two metal electrodes connected to the secondary of an induction coil
options.D: use of two non-metal rods connected to a dc voltage

explanation: According to the answer key, option D is the correct method described.

subject: Physics
text: X-rays can be used in the study of crystal structure because they ___.
explanation: X-rays have very short wavelengths comparable to atomic spacing in crystals, allowing diffraction studies.

subject: Physics
text: Which of the following is an advantage of the capacitor in an induction coil?

options.A: the presence of the capacitor is to slow down the fall of current in the primary coil at the instant of break
options.B: to produce low voltage from a high voltage for use
options.C: to provide sparks required to ignite the petrol air mixture in car cylinder
options.D: this is to prevent the induced emf in the primary coil from rising as to cause unwanted spark

explanation: The answer key gives option B as the advantage of the capacitor in an induction coil.

subject: Physics
text: Lines of force (i) are continuous in any region with charges (ii) cross each other (iii) indicate the direction of the field (iv) start and end on negative charge only. Which of the statements above are NOT correct?
explanation: Lines of force never cross, and they start on positive and end on negative charges.

subject: Physics
text: In an alternating circuit theory, the units of impedance, r.m.s voltage and resonance frequency are respectively equal to ___.
explanation: Impedance is measured in ohms, voltage in volts, and frequency in hertz.

subject: About RSU
text: The head of Department, Dean of Faculty, Faculty Examination Officer, and Secretary to the Faculty Board retain copies of the results of all students to avoid unofficial releases of results.
explanation: According to the answer key, this statement is true.

subject: About RSU
text: The departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the ___.
explanation: The answer key indicates that the departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the faculty examination officer.

subject: About RSU
text: A student in a four-year degree programme in the Rivers State University who is unable to complete his programme in 12 semesters may be allowed an additional ___ semesters, but shall be eligible to a ___ degree.
explanation: The answer key gives 2 additional semesters and a Pass degree.

subject: About RSU
text: The Rivers State University is the only university in Nigeria that offers first degree programmes in ___ and ___.

options.C: land surveying and aeronautical telecommunication engineering

explanation: According to the answer key, RSU uniquely offers first degree programmes in marine engineering and secretarial administration.

subject: About RSU
text: What is the likely punishment meted on a student who destroys possible evidence for an examination malpractice?
explanation: Destroying evidence of examination malpractice is a serious offence that typically leads to dismissal.

subject: Current Affairs
text: Which country was the last to win independence from colonial rule?
explanation: According to the answer key, Angola is the correct choice among the options given.

subject: Current Affairs
text: A country made up of semi-autonomous units is called ___.
explanation: A confederation is a union of semi-autonomous states or units.

subject: Current Affairs
text: What is the ultimate aim of political parties?

options.B: to be opposition to the government in power
options.C: to train citizens on political participation

explanation: Political parties are primarily formed to acquire and exercise political power.

subject: Current Affairs
text: What system of Government is political power inherited?
explanation: In a monarchy, political power is usually inherited.

subject: CRS
text: “But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of ___”
explanation: Jesus sent his disciples to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

subject: CRS
text: The following are qualities of a good citizen except a good citizen should ___.

options.A: pay taxes, rates, levies and electricity bills
options.B: question all orders constituted by the authorities before carrying them out
options.C: go out to cast his vote at elections
options.D: learn to express his grievances through dialogue and not through violence

explanation: A good citizen obeys lawful authority; questioning every order before obeying is the exception.

subject: CRS
text: How did God convince Jeremiah of his divine call?

options.B: He spoke to him directly from heaven
options.C: He sent a prophet to inform him
options.E: He gave him a vision of a burning bush

explanation: God touched Jeremiah’s mouth and put his words into it.

subject: CRS
text: Paul considered himself unfit to be called an apostle because he ___.

options.B: was the least among the Jewish race
options.E: was not one of the twelve disciples

explanation: Paul persecuted the church before his conversion, so he felt unworthy of the title apostle.

subject: CRS
text: “I will; be clean”, Jesus made this statement at the healing of ___.

options.A: the woman with the issue of blood

explanation: Jesus healed a leper and said, “I will; be clean.”

subject: CRS
text: The close of the Old Testament and the beginning of the New Testament is called the ___ period.
explanation: According to the answer key, this period is called the eschatological period.
